title: daucus rupestris
intro: Daucus rupestris, also known as wild carrot or rock carrot, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which make it useful in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and soothe digestive issues such as diarrhea and indigestion. Daucus rupestris can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and poultices, to treat various health conditions.
